subject.label: Initiative & Goal Priority Tier — time-sensitive gate before backlog

title: Initiative & Goal Priority Tier — time-sensitive gate before backlog
what: Defines a two-tier dispatch priority system. Tier 0 (initiative and goal work) is exhausted before Tier 1 (backlog). Any lug affiliated with an initiative in an active lifecycle state is Tier 0. Pure backlog lugs are Tier 1. Sort key across all dispatch paths: (priority_tier, urgency_tier, -roi, wave). This spec doubles as the runtime config — tools read the config block to determine which lifecycle states qualify for Tier 0. To tune the system, edit this file.
why: Without this gate, a high-ROI backlog task can silently outrank approved initiative work. The 3x focus_lock multiplier only helps within a single focus-locked initiative; it provides no hard ordering guarantee over backlog as a class. Initiatives represent the user's strategic commitments — their lugs should complete ASAP, not compete on ROI alone.

config._note: Tools read this block at runtime. To tune the tier model: edit these fields. Do not edit the Python constants.

steps_summary: 1. Load config.tier0_lifecycle_states from this spec lug at scoring startup (fallback: ['approved','active','measuring'] if lug missing)
2. Build affiliation_map {epic_id: initiative_id} — one pass over initiatives/index.json, filtered to initiatives whose lifecycle_state is in tier0_lifecycle_states
3. Per lug: priority_tier=0 if lug.parent_epic (or lug.epic_id) is in affiliation_map, else fallback_tier (1)
4. Lugs with no parent_epic field: priority_tier = fallback_tier
5. Sort key across all dispatch paths: (priority_tier, urgency_tier, -roi, wave)
6. When all Tier 0 lugs are blocked/stalled: Ozi falls through to Tier 1 automatically — no special flag needed
7. focus_lock 3x ROI boost is preserved unchanged — it controls ordering within Tier 0

tier0_qualification.approved: Committed initiative — user has approved this work, do ASAP
tier0_qualification.active: In-flight initiative — actively being worked, do ASAP
tier0_qualification.measuring: Epics complete, in measurement window — still time-sensitive until hypothesis confirmed

tier0_exclusions.proposed: Not yet committed — backlog treatment appropriate

how.goal_chains: When spec-goal-chain-v1 is implemented, goal chain lugs are assigned priority_tier = config.goal_chain_tier (0) directly, bypassing the epic→initiative lookup.

constraints: Lugs without parent_epic default to fallback_tier (1) — never false-promote to Tier 0
initiative_id must be set on epic lugs for the lookup chain to work — epics missing initiative_id land in Tier 1
affiliation_map is built once per scoring run, not per-lug — O(n) total cost
If initiatives/index.json is malformed JSON: return {} and continue — scoring still runs, all lugs land in Tier 1
If this spec lug file is missing or malformed: tools fall back to hardcoded defaults (no crash)
perceive: Read this spec. Read WAI-Spoke/initiatives/index.json — confirm valid JSON and all initiative lifecycle_states. Check each epic in each initiative for initiative_id field. Read tools/score_backlog.py, tools/spoke_expediter.py, tools/ozi_headless.py, tools/ozi_autopilot.py to understand current sort key implementations before patching.
execute: Deliver impl lugs to /home/mario/projects/basher/WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/. Ensure all harness epics have initiative_id: harness-fleet-migration. Update templates/commands/wai-initiative.md with Priority Contract section.
verify: python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('WAI-Spoke/initiatives/index.json'))" passes. Each open/in_progress epic referenced by an approved/active initiative has initiative_id set. After score_backlog.py runs: WAI-State.json _work_queue.items contain priority_tier field and tier0_count > 0. Output shows INITIATIVE (Tier 0) / BACKLOG (Tier 1) band headers.
